campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's financial activity and spending from July 2025, including how the city's investments performed that month. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options across Campbell, looking at ways to better connect different types of transit like buses, biking, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and city finances
Treasurer's monthly financial report
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan - improving transit, bike, and pedestrian connections

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could speak about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing public input, the council introduced an ordinance (a new city law) and skipped the usual first formal reading to speed up the process. The council then directed staff to put this ordinance on the consent calendar so it can be approved at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community feedback
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Ordinance scheduled for approval at next council meeting
Expedited process used to move legislation forward

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve meeting minutes from various March sessions. The council also authorized the Town Manager to move forward with a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details weren't fully detailed in the agenda provided.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute contract

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José's City Council met to check on progress with programs that help kids and young people, including youth empowerment initiatives and summer safety programs. They also reviewed how the city is enforcing codes and discussed plans to improve the library system for residents.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and youth intervention programs annual updates
Safe Summer Initiative Grant to keep kids engaged during summer months
City code enforcement performance and violations
Library facility improvements and customer experience plans

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate either in person or online through Zoom. Residents who wanted to speak during the meeting could join the video call or call in by phone and use the 'raise hand' feature to request time to comment on specific agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom or phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for online participants
Meeting conducted with hybrid in-person and remote options

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the official notes from their previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by three Human Relations Commissioners
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Flag salute ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 8, 2026, where they discussed several community programs including a food initiative called Budget and Bites, heard from a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and presented information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved meeting minutes from their previous session and opened the floor for residents to speak about any local issues.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites food program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City of Cupertino Summer Jobs program
Public comment period for residents
Approval of previous meeting minutes

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using both in-person and online options so residents could participate however works best for them. The council is making it easy for people to join meetings remotely through Zoom or phone, and residents can submit written comments before meetings through the city's website.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation options via Zoom and phone dial-in
Written public comments accepted before meetings through eComment system
City Council agenda and participation details on Legistar website

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review their spending plans and budget strategies for the next several years, approve council meeting records, and make changes to how the city fills open council positions. Council members also shared new items they want to work on and reviewed upcoming meeting schedules.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's 5-year budget and spending plan (2025-2030)
Changes to the process for filling vacant city council seats
New projects and initiatives council members want to prioritize
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the city's gas utility budget and rates for fiscal year 2027. The council reviewed and approved changes to residential and commercial natural gas rates, along with plans for reserve funds and transfers to the general fund.
keyTopics: Natural gas rate increases for residential customers (G-1 schedule)
Commercial and large business gas service rate changes (G-2 and G-3 schedules)
Financial reserves and budget transfers for the gas utility
Approval of the fiscal year 2027 gas utility financial forecast
